input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the wheels are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule V.1.2 specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.1.2
            TITLE: wheelbase

            CONTENT:
            rule V.1.2: Wheelbase
V.1.2 Wheelbase 
The vehicle must have a minimum wheelbase of 1525 mm

final_answer:
Explanation:The FSAE rule V.1.2 requires a minimum wheelbase of 1525 mm. The engineering drawing provides a primary dimension of 3202.4 mm across the top. Assuming this dimension represents the wheelbase (the distance between the centers of the front and rear axles), we compare this measured value to the minimum requirement. Since 3202.4 mm is greater than the minimum required wheelbase of 1525 mm, the design complies with Rule V.1.2.

Answer: Yes
